behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all four modified files, the extraction function requirements (set/frozenset return, lru_cache, variable names like messages/bos_token/custom_var/num_frames/fps), and the processor_kwargs parameter.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes requirements (extraction function, caching, kwarg separation) but doesn't specify exact function name (_get_template_variables) or implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests discover the extraction function dynamically by iterating dir() and testing behavior, not by asserting on the specific name _get_template_variables, so alternative implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute Python subprocesses that import modules and call functions, checking returned sets and behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual extraction returning specific variable sets from parsed Jinja templates including excluding loop variables — stubs returning empty/default would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover extraction, caching, dynamic behavior, kwarg separation, and syntax checks matching the instruction requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass) and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code behavior, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names consistent across instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction lists files to modify at a file level, which is part of the task scope, not line-level hints.
